Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For each case_id we need to get the ordered sequence of activities This can be done by concatenating the activity names in timestamp order perhaps using STRING_AGG with ORDER BY timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then we need to group by that variant_seq to count the number of cases per variant We will get a table of variant_seq and case_count

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Determine the top K variants by frequency We need to define K as a parameter In the query we can use a placeholder like or a variable

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The problem says top K variants by their frequency so we can use a window function to rank variants by count descending then pick those with rank less than or equal to K

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The simplest is to compute the counts order by count descending limit K and then join But if there is a tie for the Kth place we might miss some variants The problem statement does not specify handling ties so we can assume we want exactly K variants

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to assign each case to a variant Then we need to find the top K variants by case count Then we need to get all events for cases that belong to any of those top K variants

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: WITH case_variants AS SELECT case_id STRING_AGG activity ORDER BY timestamp AS variant_seq FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: variant_counts AS SELECT variant_seq COUNT as case_count FROM case_variants GROUP BY variant_seq

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: I will use a method that picks exactly K variants by using ROW_NUMBER ordered by count descending and then by variant_seq to break ties

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to return events from the original event_log for cases that are in these top K variants So we need to join case_variants which has case_id and variant_seq with the ranked_variants to get the case_ids that are in top K variants Then join with event_log to get events

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Alternatively we can do WITH case_variants AS SELECT case_id STRING_AGG activity ORDER BY timestamp AS variant_seq FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id variant_counts AS SELECT variant_seq COUNT as case_count FROM case_variants GROUP BY variant_seq top_k_variants AS SELECT variant_seq FROM variant_counts ORDER BY case_count DESC variant_seq LIMIT K SELECT el FROM event_log el JOIN case_variants cv ON el case_id equals cv case_id JOIN top_k_variants tk ON cv variant_seq equals tk variant_seq ORDER BY el case_id el timestamp

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Using a string is fine Alternatively we could use an array ARRAY_AGG activity ORDER BY timestamp That might be more robust because it does not rely on a delimiter

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Arrays are safer because they avoid delimiter issues and can be used in GROUP BY and compared for equality

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: WITH case_variants AS SELECT case_id ARRAY_AGG activity ORDER BY timestamp AS variant_seq FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id variant_counts AS SELECT variant_seq COUNT as case_count FROM case_variants GROUP BY variant_seq top_k_variants AS SELECT variant_seq FROM variant_counts ORDER BY case_count DESC variant_seq LIMIT 1 SELECT el FROM event_log el JOIN case_variants cv ON el case_id equals cv case_id JOIN top_k_variants tk ON cv variant_seq equals tk variant_seq ORDER BY el case_id el timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This query will return all events for cases that are in the top K variants The top_k_variants CTE selects the variant_seq of the top K variants Then we join with case_variants to get the case_ids that have those variant_seq Then join with event_log to get events

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: To make the order deterministic if timestamps are equal I will add activity as a secondary sort ORDER BY timestamp activity

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: WITH case_variants AS SELECT case_id ARRAY_AGG activity ORDER BY timestamp activity AS variant_seq FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id variant_counts AS SELECT variant_seq COUNT as case_count FROM case_variants GROUP BY variant_seq ranked_variants AS SELECT variant_seq ROW_NUMBER OVER ORDER BY case_count DESC variant_seq AS rn FROM variant_counts top_k_variants AS SELECT variant_seq FROM ranked_variants WHERE rn less than or equal to 1 SELECT el FROM event_log el JOIN case_variants cv ON el case_id equals cv case_id JOIN top_k_variants tk ON cv variant_seq equals tk variant_seq ORDER BY el case_id el timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The query is complete It determines the sequence per case groups cases by variant counts cases per variant identifies the top K variants and returns all events for cases belonging to those variants
